questions.q: E. C. George Sudarshan is best known for his work in which field of science?
questions.explanation: Sudarshan was a theoretical physicist celebrated for contributions to particle physics and quantum optics.

questions.q: In which country was E. C. George Sudarshan born?
questions.explanation: Sudarshan was born in 1931 in Kerala, India.

questions.q: Sudarshan co-developed a famous theory describing which of the four fundamental forces?
questions.explanation: He co-formulated the V-A (vector minus axial-vector) theory of the weak interaction.

questions.q: The 'V-A' theory that Sudarshan helped create describes which kind of interaction?
questions.explanation: The V-A theory is the foundational description of the weak nuclear force responsible for processes like beta decay.

questions.q: Besides particle physics, Sudarshan made landmark contributions to which other area of physics?
questions.explanation: Sudarshan founded much of the modern quantum theory of optical coherence.

questions.q: Sudarshan is often described in his card subtitle as which kind of figure?
questions.explanation: He is widely remembered as an overlooked genius, repeatedly passed over for the Nobel Prize.

questions.q: The letters 'V' and 'A' in Sudarshan's V-A theory stand for which terms?
questions.explanation: V-A refers to the vector minus axial-vector structure of the weak interaction's coupling.

questions.q: Which prestigious prize did Sudarshan never receive despite many believing he deserved it?
questions.explanation: Sudarshan was nominated multiple times but never awarded the Nobel Prize, a frequent point of controversy.

questions.q: Sudarshan spent much of his American academic career as a professor at which university in Texas?
options: The University of Texas at Austin
questions.explanation: He was a long-serving professor of physics at the University of Texas at Austin.

questions.q: Sudarshan's work spanned both particle physics and the physics of which everyday phenomenon?
questions.explanation: His quantum theory of optical coherence concerns the fundamental quantum nature of light.

questions.q: With which physicist did Sudarshan co-author the V-A theory of the weak interaction in 1957-58?
questions.explanation: Sudarshan developed the V-A theory together with his doctoral adviser Robert Marshak.

questions.q: The representation of quantum states of light that bears Sudarshan's name is commonly called the:
questions.explanation: The Sudarshan-Glauber P-representation expresses quantum optical states in terms of coherent states.

questions.q: In 2005, the Nobel Prize in Physics for quantum optical coherence went to Roy Glauber, sparking controversy because many felt who had been overlooked?
questions.explanation: Many physicists protested that Sudarshan's foundational work on optical coherence was unjustly omitted from the 2005 Nobel.

questions.q: Sudarshan helped propose the existence of hypothetical particles that travel faster than light, known as:
questions.explanation: Sudarshan was a leading proponent of tachyons, hypothetical superluminal particles.

questions.q: A counterintuitive quantum phenomenon Sudarshan co-described, in which frequent observation freezes a system's evolution, is the:
questions.explanation: With B. Misra, Sudarshan formulated the quantum Zeno effect, where continuous measurement inhibits a system's change.

questions.q: The V-A theory established that the weak interaction violates which symmetry?
questions.explanation: The V-A structure built in maximal parity violation, reflecting that the weak force distinguishes left from right.

questions.q: Sudarshan earned his PhD in the United States from which university?
questions.explanation: He completed his doctorate at the University of Rochester under Robert Marshak.

questions.q: In which broad century did Sudarshan carry out his most influential research?
questions.explanation: Sudarshan's major work, beginning in the 1950s, took place in the second half of the 20th century.

questions.q: Before moving to the University of Texas, Sudarshan held a long professorship at which New York university?
questions.explanation: He was a professor at Syracuse University before joining the University of Texas at Austin.

questions.q: Sudarshan's 'diagonal' P-representation was significant because it showed that:
options: Any quantum optical state can be described using coherent states
questions.explanation: His diagonal representation demonstrated that arbitrary quantum states of light can be expanded over coherent states.

questions.q: Which Indian civilian honour was conferred on Sudarshan by the Government of India?
questions.explanation: Sudarshan was awarded the Padma Vibhushan, one of India's highest civilian honours.

questions.q: The weak-interaction theory Sudarshan and Marshak proposed is sometimes called the 'universal' theory of the weak force because it:
options: Applied a single common form to all weak processes
questions.explanation: The V-A theory provided a single universal current-current form covering all known weak decays.

questions.q: Sudarshan's contributions to the dynamics of open quantum systems are reflected in which named master equation?
questions.explanation: The GKSL (Lindblad) master equation, bearing Sudarshan's name, governs the evolution of open quantum systems.

questions.q: The V-A theory of Sudarshan and Marshak was published around the same time that Feynman and Gell-Mann independently proposed an equivalent theory. In which years did this occur?
questions.explanation: Both formulations of the V-A weak-interaction theory appeared in 1957-58.

questions.q: The result establishing that Sudarshan's P-representation and Glauber's coherent-state description are mathematically equivalent is sometimes called the:
questions.explanation: The optical equivalence theorem links Sudarshan's diagonal P-representation to the description of optical coherence.

questions.q: Sudarshan introduced the concept of dynamical maps to describe which process in quantum systems?
options: The collapse of a black hole
Evolution of a system interacting with its environment
questions.explanation: His work on completely positive dynamical maps describes how open quantum systems evolve while coupled to an environment.

questions.q: Negative values appearing in Sudarshan's P-function are used as a signature of what property of light?
questions.explanation: A P-function that fails to be a true probability distribution signals genuinely nonclassical states of light.

questions.q: Sudarshan's collaborator on the quantum Zeno effect, with whom he published the 1977 paper, was:
questions.explanation: Sudarshan and B. Misra introduced and named the quantum Zeno effect (Zeno's paradox in quantum theory) in 1977.

questions.q: The V-A theory replaced an earlier, incorrect assumption that the weak interaction had which coupling structure?
questions.explanation: Sudarshan and Marshak argued against the then-favoured scalar-tensor couplings in favour of vector minus axial-vector.

questions.q: Sudarshan also contributed a group-theoretic approach to particle classification, co-authoring an influential text on which subject?
options: Classical and quantum mechanics, and symmetry/group theory in physics
questions.explanation: Sudarshan co-authored noted texts on the role of symmetry and group theory in classical and quantum dynamics.
